name: Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop
name_original: Sierras de Pàndols-Cavalls – circular desde Bot por Gandesa y Prat del Compte
description: Mountain bike the moderate 20.3-mile Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop, featuring greenway tunnels, viaducts, and scenic vi
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Bot are primarily characterized by the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, a network of greenways built on former railway lines. This region features a rural landscape with varied terrain, including sections that pass through tunnels and over viaducts. The area offers a mix of flat, accessible paths and routes with notable elevation changes, providing diverse mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Bot The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop, a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical paths with scenic greenway sections. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bot Station Bar (Railbus) loop from Horta de Sant Joan, an easy 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This route offers gentle gradients along the greenway, suitable for a relaxed ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bridge Over the Algars River loop from Antiga Estació de Prat de Comte, a 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Bot is defined by its greenways, former railway lines, and the surrounding rural scenery. The network offers options for easy rides on converted rail paths as well as more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Bot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Bot?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Bot, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find routes suitable for various skill levels, from easy greenways to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Bot?
A: Yes, Bot offers 8 easy mountain bike trails. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bot Station Bar (Railbus) loop from Horta de Sant Joan&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.3 miles (18.2 km) long and features gentle gradients along the greenway.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on mountain bike trails around Bot?
A: Mountain bike trails around Bot are primarily characterized by the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, a network of greenways built on former railway lines. You'll experience a rural landscape with varied terrain, including sections that pass through tunnels and over viaducts. The area offers a mix of flat, accessible paths and routes with notable elevation changes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Bot?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique experience of riding along the converted railway lines of the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, the varied rural scenery, and the options for both relaxed and challenging rides.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Bot?
A: Yes, many routes around Bot are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945418695/ruta-de-la-pau-and-via-verda-de-la-terra-alta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) circular trail that combines historical paths with scenic greenway sections.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with cafes or places to rest in Bot?
A: Yes, some routes pass by convenient spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight along the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, offering a place to stop and refresh during your ride.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often follow the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta&lt;/a&gt;, where you can see an &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railway viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt;, known for its thermal springs, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrassó Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bridge Over the Algars River loop from Antiga Estació de Prat de Comte&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 5 hours.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints in Bot?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to showcase the region's beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40370165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – View of Gandesa loop from Poblat ibèric del Coll del Moro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.6 km (13.4 miles) trail specifically highlighting scenic vistas, including views of Gandesa.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Bot?
A: The longest trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bridge Over the Algars River loop from Antiga Estació de Prat de Comte&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 48.2 miles (77.6 km) and is considered difficult.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by natural water features like waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrassó Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the area, and some trails may pass nearby or offer access to it.
tips: Old railway station in the municipality of Bot, in Terra Alta, which belonged to the Val de Zafán railway. The railway complex that connected La Puebla de Híjar, in the province of Teruel, with Tortosa, in Tarragona, has been replaced by a greenway. In the old Bot station there is currently a bar where you can stop during the route.
This tunnel connects Terra Alta with Tarragona in the Natural Park of Los Puertos.

Sanctuary located next to the Terra Alta Greenway within the area of the Pàndols-Cavalls mountains. The building, built in the 19th century, has a group of baroque-style buildings around it. The name of the sanctuary comes from the mineral-medicinal water source located to the left of the Canaleta river.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near Bot, which is a strategic point for accessing the Via Verda de la Terra Alta. You can often find parking options in or around the towns connected by the greenway, such as Bot or Horta de Sant Joan. Old railway stations along the route are sometimes converted into facilities that may offer parking.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Via Verda de la Terra Alta is generally accessible, specific rules for dogs can vary. It's best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking this route. The weather is milder, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful with blooming flora or autumn colors. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be less pleasant during peak heat.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Ruta de la Pau and Via Verda de la Terra Alta Loop itself. It's a public greenway and trail system.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking within the Serres de Pàndols-Cavalls area?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking in the Serres de Pàndols-Cavalls area, it's important to stick to marked trails, respect private property, and avoid disturbing wildlife. As a natural area, take all your rubbish with you and be mindful of fire risks, especially during dry seasons. Always yield to hikers and other trail users.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The core of the route, the Via Verda de la Terra Alta, is generally accessible and suitable for families and beginners due to its relatively consistent surface and gentle gradient. However, this specific loop includes additional sections that contribute to its 'moderate' difficulty, involving more elevation gain and potentially unpaved, more challenging segments. Beginners with good fitness should be fine, but families with very young children might prefer shorter, easier sections of the Via Verda.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse. A significant portion follows the Via Verda de la Terra Alta, which is a former railway line, offering a mix of mostly paved and some unpaved sections. You'll encounter numerous tunnels and viaducts. The loop also ventures into varied landscapes including wooded areas, rural environments, olive groves, almond trees, and vineyards, with some segments potentially being more challenging and unpaved.

faqs.question: What interesting sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several highlights. You'll pass through the Tunnel of the Terra Alta Greenway, experience the unique 'aerial character' of the viaducts, and can visit the beautiful Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fontcalda. The trail also traverses a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, showcasing impressive mountain landscapes and cultivated fields.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find amenities along the route, particularly in the towns it connects. The Bot Station Bar (Railbus) is a notable spot. Old railway stations are sometimes converted into restaurants or accommodations, providing convenient stops for refreshments.

faqs.question: Are there any specific equipment recommendations for the tunnels on the Via Verda?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Via Verda de la Terra Alta features numerous tunnels, some of which are unlit, including one that is 739 meters long. It is highly recommended to carry lights with spare batteries to safely navigate these sections.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails in the region?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop intersects with several other official trails. Notable connections include sections of the El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Via Verda - Corbera d'Ebre, TransCatalunya. De Ponent, and the Camí Natural de la Terra Alta.

faqs.question: Are there any natural swimming spots near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is known for natural attractions. You can find natural swimming spots, such as those near the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fontcalda, which is a highlight along the route.
